description_long: This object is a handheld two-way radio transceiver, commonly known as a walkie-talkie. It features a long, slender antenna extending from the top, which is essential for signal transmission and reception. The front face displays a small digital screen, a numeric keypad, and several physical buttons for channel selection and operation. The top section includes two rotary knobs, likely for power/volume and channel adjustment, as well as a speaker grille. The casing is made of durable black plastic, designed to withstand rough handling in urban environments. The back side has a metallic battery compartment cover, indicating a removable battery for easy replacement.
description: A black handheld walkie-talkie with a long antenna, digital display, keypad, and rotary knobs. Rugged plastic casing and a metallic battery cover on the back.
description_view_0: Front view showing the digital display, keypad, speaker grille, and rotary knobs on top.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the slim profile and side buttons for additional controls.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the metallic battery compartment cover and the back of the antenna.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing a flat surface with minimal visible features.
